steps.title: Tie size to access
steps.content: The researchers think PCFG whales' smaller maximum size may be an adaptation to resource opportunities in their foraging range. The strongest support would show that being smaller helps those whales reach food or habitat that larger ENP whales cannot use.

steps.title: Avoid unrelated differences
steps.content: A concerns ports and urban populations, not resources. B reports body-size trends without explaining an adaptive advantage. D identifies different prey, but it does not show why smaller bodies matter. A shallow kelp-bed habitat inaccessible to larger whales directly links size to foraging opportunity. The answer is C.
